conversation.content:
Reconcile the following columns. Document has 11 pages. Sources are anonymous (A and B).

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: Add-on Treatment
Definition: What is the name of additional therapy or intervention given in combination with the primary treatment in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Darolutamide" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The trial compares darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel with placebo + ADT + docetaxel, indicating darolutamide is the additional agent being studied."
  B: value="Androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="From Page 1, METHODS section: 'darolutamide ... or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.'"

---
Column 2: COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ
Definition: What is the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)?.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document describes the trial design and conduct but does not provide an overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) for the RCT itself."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document does not explicitly state the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)."

---
Column 3: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Definition: What the type or category of therapeutic agent used in the first experimental arm of the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Androgen-receptor inhibitor, hormonal therapy, chemotherapy" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Darolutamide is an androgen-receptor inhibitor (page 1, abstract). The regimen includes androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT), which is hormonal therapy, and docetaxel, which is chemotherapy (page 1, abstract)."
  B: value="Androgen-receptor inhibitor" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="From Page 1, BACKGROUND section: 'Darolutamide is a potent androgen-receptor inhibitor'."

---
Column 4: Control Arm
Definition: What the name of the arm in the clinical trial that does not receive the experimental treatment, serving as a comparison group?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Placebo + Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) + Docetaxel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Patients were randomly assigned to receive darolutamide or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel (page 1, abstract)."
  B: value="Placebo + androgen-deprivation therapy + docetaxel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="From Page 1, METHODS section: 'matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.'"

---
Column 5: Control Arm - N
Definition: What the total number of participants included in the control arm of the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'. If the trial contains multiple control arms, report for all separately
  A: value="655" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The primary analysis involved 1306 patients (651 in the darolutamide group and 655 in the placebo group) (page 1, abstract)."
  B: value="655" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="From Page 1, RESULTS section: '1306 patients (651 in the darolutamide group and 655 in the placebo group)'."

---
Column 6: Median Follow-Up Duration (mo)
Definition: What the median length of time, measured in months, that participants in the clinical trial were followed up or monitored after the start of the trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="43.7 months (darolutamide group) and 42.4 months (placebo group)" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="The median follow-up for overall survival was 43.7 months in the darolutamide group and 42.4 months in the placebo group (page 4, Results - Patients)."
  B: value="Darolutamide group: 43.7 months; Placebo group: 42.4 months" | page=4 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 4 states, 'The median follow-up for overall survival was 43.7 months in the darolutamide group and 42.4 months in the placebo group.'"

---
Column 7: Number of Arms Included
Definition: What is the total count of experimental treatment arms and control arms included in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="2" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Patients were randomly assigned in a 1:1 ratio to receive darolutamide or matching placebo (page 1, abstract), indicating two arms."
  B: value="2" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="From Page 1, METHODS section: 'randomly assigned patients ... in a 1:1 ratio to receive darolutamide ... or matching placebo'."

---
Column 8: Primary Endpoint(s)
Definition: What the main outcome(s) or objective(s) that the clinical trial is designed to measure or achieve?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Overall survival" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The primary end point was overall survival (page 1, abstract; page 3, End Points)."
  B: value="Overall survival" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="From Page 1, METHODS section: 'The primary end point was overall survival.'"

---
Column 9: Quality of Life Scale
Definition: What a measure of the participants' overall well-being and satisfaction with their health status, as assessed through a standardized questionnaire or survey, reflecting their perceived quality of life during the clinical trial?.
  A: value="Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire" | page=3 | modality=text | reasoning="Pain was assessed with the use of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, and disease-related physical symptoms were assessed with the use of the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire (page 3, Assessmen"
  B: value="Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire; Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire" | page=3 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 3, ASSESSMENTS section, states: 'Pain was assessed with the use of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, and disease-related physical symptoms were assessed with the use of the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17"

---
Column 10: Quality of Life reported
Definition: Does the clinical trial report results on the quality of life of participants? Answer Yes or No
  A: value="Yes" | page=3 | modality=text | reasoning="Disease-related physical symptoms were assessed using a standardized questionnaire, and time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms was a secondary endpoint (page 3, End Points; page 6, Table 2)."
  B: value="Yes" | page=3 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 3, ASSESSMENTS section, mentions the use of 'Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire' and 'Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire' to assess pain and disease-related physical symptoms, which are componen"

---
Column 11: Secondary Endpoint(s)
Definition: What the secondary outcome(s) or objective(s) of the clinical trial, in addition to the primary endpoint, that are being measured or evaluated?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to a first symptomatic skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic " | page=3 | modality=text | reasoning="The secondary end points were time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to a first symptomatic skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical "

---
Column 12: Total Participants - N
Definition: What are the total number of participants included in the clinical trial? These include only participants who were inlcuded in either the treatment or control arm of the clinical trial. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.If multiple comparative trials are mentioned, report for all separately
  A: value="1306" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The primary analysis involved 1306 patients (page 1, abstract). A total of 1306 patients underwent randomization (page 4, Results - Patients)."
  B: value="1306" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="From Page 1, RESULTS section: 'The primary analysis involved 1306 patients'."

---
Column 13: Treatment Arm - N
Definition: What are the total number of participants included in the experimental arm of the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'. If the trial contains multiple treatment arms, report for all separately
  A: value="651" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The primary analysis involved 1306 patients (651 in the darolutamide group and 655 in the placebo group) (page 1, abstract)."

---
Column 14: Treatment Arm 1 Regimen
Definition: What the specific treatment protocol or regimen used in the experimental arms of the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the treament regimen used ; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Darolutamide (600 mg twice daily) + Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) + Docetaxel (six cycles of 75 mg per square meter)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Patients were randomly assigned to receive darolutamide (at a dose of 600 mg [two 300-mg tablets] twice daily) or matching placebo, both in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel (page 1, abstract). Docetaxel was given as six cycles of 75 mg per square meter (page 3, Patients an"
  B: value="Darolutamide (600 mg twice daily) + androgen-deprivation therapy + docetaxel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="From Page 1, METHODS section: 'darolutamide (at a dose of 600 mg [two 300-mg tablets] twice daily) ... in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.'"

---
Column 15: Treatment Arm(s)
Definition: What name of the experimental arm or arms in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Patients were randomly assigned to receive darolutamide... in combination with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel (page 1, abstract)."

response.formatted_chunks:
[Page 1]
## Page 1

*The* NEW ENGLAND JOURNAL *of* MEDICINE



ORIGINAL ARTICLE



Darolutamide and Survival in Metastatic,
Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer

Matthew R. Smith, M.D., Ph.D., Maha Hussain, M.D., Fred Saad, M.D.,
Karim Fizazi, M.D., Ph.D., Cora N. Sternberg, M.D., E. David Crawford, M.D.,
Evgeny Kopyltsov, M.D., Chandler H. Park, M.D., Boris Alekseev, M.D.,
Álvaro Montesa-Pino, M.D., Dingwei Ye, M.D., Francis Parnis, M.B., B.S.,
Felipe Cruz, M.D., Teuvo L.J. Tammela, M.D., Ph.D., Hiroyoshi Suzuki, M.D., Ph.D.,
Tapio Utriainen, M.D., Cheng Fu, M.D., Motohide Uemura, M.D., Ph.D.,
María J. Méndez-Vidal, M.D., Benjamin L. Maughan, M.D., Pharm.D.,
Heikki Joensuu, M.D., Silke Thiele, M.D., Rui Li, M.S., Iris Kuss, M.D.,
and Bertrand Tombal, M.D., Ph.D., for the ARASENS Trial Investigators*



ABSTRACT



## BACKGROUND
Darolutamide is a potent androgen-receptor inhibitor that has been associated with increased overall survival among patients with nonmetastatic, castration-resistant prostate cancer. Whether a combination of darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy, and docetaxel would increase survival among patients with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer is unknown.



METHODS
In this international, phase 3 trial, we randomly assigned patients with metastatic,
hormone-sensitive prostate cancer in a 1:1 ratio to receive darolutamide (at a dose
of 600 mg [two 300-mg tablets] twice daily) or matching placebo, both in combi-
nation with androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel. The primary end point
was overall survival.



## RESULTS
The primary analysis involved 1306 patients (651 in the darolutamide group and 655 in the placebo group); 86.1% of the patients had disease that was metastatic at the time of the initial diagnosis. At the data cutoff date for the primary analysis (October 25, 2021), the risk of death was significantly lower, by 32.5%, in the darolutamide group than in the placebo group (hazard ratio 0.68; 95% confidence interval, 0.57 to 0.80; P<0.001). Darolutamide was also associated with consistent benefits with respect to the secondary end points and prespecified subgroups. Adverse events were similar in the two groups, and the incidences of the most common adverse events (occurring in ≥10% of the patients) were highest during the overlapping docetaxel treatment period in both groups. The frequency of grade 3 or 4 adverse events was 66.1% in the darolutamide group and 63.5% in the placebo group; neutropenia was the most common grade 3 or 4 adverse event (in 33.7% and 34.2%, respectively).



CONCLUSIONS
In this trial involving patients with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, overall survival was significantly longer with the combination of darolutamide, androgen-deprivation therapy, and docetaxel than with placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel, and the addition of darolutamide led to improvement in key secondary end points. The frequency of adverse events was similar in the two groups. (Funded by Bayer and Orion Pharma; ARASENS ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT02799602.)



The authors' affiliations are listed in the
Appendix. Dr. Smith can be contacted at
mrsmith@mgh.harvard.edu or at the
Genitourinary Malignancies Program,
Massachusetts General Hospital Cancer
Center and Harvard Medical School,
Yawkey 7030, 55 Fruit St., Boston, MA
02114.



*A full list of investigators in the ARASENS trial is provided in the Supplementary Appendix, available at NEJM.org.



This article was published on February
17, 2022, at NEJM.org.



DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa2119115
Copyright © 2022 Massachusetts Medical Society.



N ENGL J MED NEJM.ORG



1



The New England Journal of Medicine
Downloaded from nejm.org by Syed Arsalan Ahmed NAQVI on February 17, 2022. For personal use only. No other uses without permission.
